stories.headline: NASA's Curiosity Rover Finds Most Diverse Set of Organic Molecules Ever Detected on Mars
stories.summary: A rock drilled by NASA's Curiosity rover in 2020 has yielded the greatest diversity of organic (carbon-containing) molecules ever found on Mars, with 21 molecules identified — seven of them detected on the Red Planet for the first time. Among the newly found molecules is a nitrogen heterocycle, a ring structure considered a chemical predecessor to RNA and DNA, the building blocks of genetic information. Scientists cannot yet say whether life or geology created these molecules, but the discovery strengthens the case that ancient Mars had the right chemistry to support life.
stories.bigWordDef: Carbon-containing chemical compounds that are the building blocks of life — found in all living things on Earth, and now increasingly on Mars too!
stories.thinkQuestion: If scientists one day confirm that life once existed on Mars, how do you think that discovery would change the way humans think about our place in the universe?
stories.videoQuery: NASA Curiosity Mars organic molecules life explained
stories.sourceQuote: Of the 21 carbon-containing molecules identified in the sample, seven of them were detected for the first time on Mars.

stories.headline: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire at Pakistan's Request as Peace Talks Remain Uncertain
stories.summary: President Trump extended the two-week US-Iran ceasefire just hours before it was set to expire, after Pakistan requested more time for Iranian officials to present a unified proposal. The key sticking points remain Iran's nuclear enrichment programme and control of the Strait of Hormuz, through which around 20% of the world's crude oil and natural gas passes. Vice President Vance's planned trip to Islamabad was postponed, with Iran signalling it would not send a delegation for immediate talks.
stories.bigWordDef: A temporary agreement between two fighting sides to stop all attacks and give peace talks a chance — like pressing 'pause' on a war.
stories.thinkQuestion: When two countries are in a conflict, what do you think makes it so hard to agree on the terms of a lasting peace deal?
stories.videoQuery: US Iran ceasefire peace talks 2026 explained
stories.sourceQuote: For Tehran, the key demands for extending the ceasefire include an end to the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports and guarantees that the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah will not resume.

stories.headline: Earth Day 2026: 'Our Power, Our Planet' Rallies a Billion People Across 190 Countries
stories.summary: Today, April 22, marks Earth Day 2026 under the theme 'Our Power, Our Planet', focusing on how individuals, communities, and governments can drive environmental progress together. The theme emphasises accelerating the shift to renewable energy, strengthening community-led sustainability efforts, and using collective action to build a cleaner future. Earth Day, first celebrated in 1970, has grown into one of the largest environmental movements in the world, now observed in more than 190 countries.
stories.bigWordDef: Using the Earth's resources in a way that doesn't use them up, so future generations can enjoy them too — like spending only what you earn and saving the rest.
stories.thinkQuestion: What is one change you could make in your daily life in Bengaluru that would help the environment, and why do you think small actions add up?
stories.videoQuery: Earth Day 2026 Our Power Our Planet theme explained
stories.sourceQuote: The theme of Earth Day 2026 is reported as "Our Power, Our Planet," focusing on the role of individuals, communities, and governments in driving environmental progress.

stories.headline: India's Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor Achieves Historic First Criticality at Kalpakkam
stories.summary: India's 500 MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam, Tamil Nadu, successfully achieved 'first criticality' on April 6, 2026, meaning a controlled nuclear fission chain reaction is now self-sustaining for the first time. The reactor, which took over two decades to build, is designed to produce more fuel than it consumes, using a blanket of Uranium-238 and eventually thorium. This marks India's entry into the second stage of its ambitious three-stage nuclear power programme aimed at long-term clean energy using India's vast thorium reserves.
stories.bigWordDef: The exact point at which a nuclear reactor's chain reaction becomes self-sustaining — like lighting a fire that keeps burning on its own without needing more matches.
stories.thinkQuestion: India has abundant thorium but limited uranium — how do you think having your own fuel source changes a country's energy independence and global position?
stories.videoQuery: India fast breeder reactor Kalpakkam criticality explained
stories.sourceQuote: Fast breeder technology serves as a vital bridge between the current fleet of Pressurized Heavy Water Reactors and future thorium-based systems, leveraging the country's abundant thorium resources for sustainable and long-term clean energy generation.

videos.title: What is the Strait of Hormuz and why is it important
videos.description: A clear explainer on why the Strait of Hormuz — a narrow waterway between Iran and Oman — is one of the most important shipping lanes on Earth, carrying nearly 20% of the world's oil. Great for understanding why the current US-Iran standoff affects fuel prices everywhere, including in India.
videos.searchQuery: Strait of Hormuz explained for students

videos.title: NASA Curiosity rover finds ancient organic molecules on Mars
videos.description: An engaging video tour of how NASA's car-sized Curiosity rover drills into Martian rocks, analyses them in its onboard lab, and sends findings back to Earth — helping scientists piece together whether ancient Mars could have supported life.
videos.searchQuery: NASA Curiosity Mars organic molecules life explained

videos.title: Earth Day 2026 🌍 | Our Power, Our Planet | Climate Crisis Warning Explained
videos.description: A short, engaging video explaining the history of Earth Day since 1970, this year's theme 'Our Power, Our Planet', and practical actions young people can take — from planting trees to reducing plastic use — to make a real difference in their communities.
videos.searchQuery: Earth Day 2026 Our Power Our Planet explained kids

infographic.caption: 🌍 Earth Day by the Numbers — April 22, 2026
infographic.fact: 1 billion people in 193+ countries participate in Earth Day · First celebrated in 1970 · 56 years of global environmental action · 21 organic molecules found on Mars by Curiosity rover this week · 135* — Abhishek Sharma's IPL runs last night
infographic.question: If 1 billion people each planted just one tree on Earth Day, how many trees would that be — and how long do you think it would take one tree to absorb 1 tonne of CO₂?
